observations.recommendation: We recommended that management ensure that only allowable expenditures shall be charged against the DF to conform with the DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1, s. 2020. Henceforth, it is advised that the Municipality strictly adhere with the provisions of the JMC especially on PPAs that are not allowed to be charged against the 20% DF otherwise these disbursements will be subjected to disallowance.

We recommended that the procuring entity furnish a copy of the contract within five (5) days upon perfection to the COA Office as required under COA Circular 2009-001, together with the bidding documents and such other documents peculiar to the contract which are deemed necessary in the conduct of auditorial review and in the technical evaluation thereof.

We recommended that management:

1.    Furnish the Office of the Auditor at the beginning of every calendar year a list of all its on-going PPAs and those that are to be implemented during the year;

2.    Inform the Office of the Auditor within ten (10) days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the program/activity that the appropriate project signboards and/or public notices are already posted; and

3.    Prepare and maintain a complete monthly Report of Publicized Government Projects/Programs/Activities containing not only those PPAs procured thru competitive bidding but also those that are procured thru other alternative modes of procurement.

We recommend that:

xxx

Management cause the closure of the establishments, the revocation of the lease contracts and ejection in case of failure to fully settle their accounts within the agreed period and apply the administrative and/or judicial actions as enumerated in Section 174 of RA 7160 in order to recover the amount due; and

The Sangguniang Bayan consider amending The Revised Economic Enterprise Code of the Municipality of Mahayag, Zamboanga del Sur to add penal provisions for non-payment of fees on time by stallholders which may include imposition of penalties, temporary closure, and revocation of contract and ejection;

xxx

xxx

We recommended the following to management:

xxx

xxx

Demand the responsible accountable officers to immediately liquidate their outstanding cash advances to avoid withholding of payment of any money due to them as provided for by COA Circular No. 97-002.

We recommended that management direct the COs and LO to turn over their collections daily and intact to the LO and Municipal Treasurer, respectively, and follow strictly the rules and regulations on the collection, deposit and recording of funds to avoid risks of loss and/or misuse of funds in their custody.

We recommended that the management require and monitor the SDOs to record cash advances and disbursements daily in the cashbook, reconcile daily the cashbook balance with the cash on hand, foot and close the books and certify the balance thereof at the end of each month pursuant to the provisions of Section 28, Volume II of the Manual on New Government Accounting System for LGUs.

We recommended that management comply with the required staffing pattern taking into consideration its service requirements and financial capability, subject to the minimum standards and guidelines prescribed by the Civil Service Commission (CSC).

Recommendation

We recommended the management to determine the status of settlement or collectability of these accounts and consider the submission of request for write-off, if warranted, following the procedures and qualifications provided under COA Circular No. 2016-005.

Recommendation

We recommended the management to require the following:
The Office of the Municipal Engineer regularly provide the Office of the Municipal Accountant with a copy of the report on the status of government projects/programs/activities. This will ensure that the Office of the Municipal Accountant stays updated on the projects being implemented for recording the CIP accounts, and reclassification to its appropriate PPE accounts upon completion; and
The Municipal Accountant reclassify the completed projects recorded under the CIP to its appropriate PPE account.

Recommendation

We recommended the management to require the Municipal Treasurer to deposit the LGU’s collection regularly and intact as prescribed and implement necessary control measures for handling cash collections and deposits to prevent exposing the limited government funds to the risk of loss or misuse.

Recommendation

We recommended the following:
Strengthen the Project Monitoring and Evaluation Division by providing capable technical personnel and ensure regular monitoring on the implementation of projects by the Municipal Engineering Office and assess the feasibility of project implementations;
The MPDC to continue reviewing the previous years’ AIPs, evaluate the feasibility of unimplemented PPAs, and propose to the Municipal Planning and Development Council to consider changes or realignments in the budget for projects which cannot be implemented or have residual balances. These funds be redirected to other viable developmental projects, subject to the approval of the Sangguniang Bayan; and
Ensure that PPAs to be funded out of the 20 percent DF are well-planned and procurement-and -implementation-ready.

Recommendation

We recommended to management the following:
Furnish the Office of the Auditor copy of the subject contracts and its supporting documents as soon as possible;
Observe the prescribed reglementary period in submission of the required documents of all future projects or programs for timely, necessary, systematic and effective review and evaluation of the project; and
Ensure compliance with the required posting of tarpaulin signboards at the project site as well as the regular update of its information based on the status of its implementation to provide the public the essential project information as required under COA Circular No. 2013-004.

Recommendation

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, as Chairman of the MDRRM Council, and the MDRRM Officer:
Maximize the utilization of the LDRRMF in order to effectively increase the level of preparedness of the municipality during natural calamities; and
Provide specific timelines and implement the PPAs under the LDRRMFIP within the said timelines.

Recommendation

We recommended that the management create and establish Monitoring and Evaluation Team in consonance with the provision of Section 5 of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 to evaluate the LGU GAD policies, programs and projects.

Recommendation

We recommended that management ensure that expenses on trainings, seminars, and planning activities are in accordance with the rates prescribed under EO No. 77.

Recommendation

We recommended that Management:
Submit justification for the procurement of expensive model/brand of cellular phone which is unnecessary expenditure pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-003 dated October 29, 2012; and
Observe prudence in the incurrence of expenditures by giving due consideration to the actual needs and requirements and by always adhering with the economic measures in every procurement activity to be undertaken.

Recommendation

We recommended the following to the LSB:
Submit quarterly and annual reports using the SEF Budget Accountability Form No. 1 (Annex B of DepEd-DBM-DILG JC No. 1, s. 2017) on SEF utilization, as prepared by the Local Accountant, to the DepEd CO, through appropriate channels, copy furnished the local sanggunian concerned and Regional Offices of the DBM and DILG, within the timeline; and
Ensure the posting of the SEF Utilization Reports at the website of the LGU, if applicable, and/or in at least three (3) conspicuous public places and the same be made not later than the 20th day after the end of each quarter as required under DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2024-021.
We recommended that Management require the Accounting Office to record expenses in their proper classification in accordance to COA Circular No. 2015-009.

We recommended that the management refrain from splitting the procurement and consolidate requisitions for one or more items needed at about or around the same time by the same or different requisitioners. This will ensure compliance with the procurement process and prevent potential irregularities in the acquisition of goods and services.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit to the Audit Team Leader the check and the cash advance voucher together with the supporting documents with covering transmittal letter in a sealed envelope within 7 days after release of check as required in the Joint Circular.

We recommended that management first secure an LTOPF for Juridical Entity should there be a necessity to acquire firearms before allocating funds for its purchase and ensure that the use of the LGU’s CF is limited only to those enumerated under Section 4.8 of the COA, DBM, DILG, GCG and DND Joint Circular No. 2015-001.

We recommended that the MPOC adhere with the 2/3 voting requirement in the release of the CF as prescribed in Sections 4.6 and 6.1.8.3.c of the COA, DBM, DILG, GCG and DND Joint Circular No. 2015-001. The results of the voting must be shown in the minutes of meetings or the MPOC Resolutions to identify who amongst the Council were in favor, against, abstained and absent.

We recommended that the management:    

1.    Issue notice of such loss to be circularized for the public information and perusal, specifying the information required under GAO Memorandum Circular No. 318 dated February 11, 1957, as reiterated by COA Circular No. 84-233 dated August 21, 1984; 

2.    Conduct an investigation and submit a report on the loss of AF indicating therein the facts of events, level of diligence or negligence of the AO and the job order personnel concerned, and comments by the immediate supervisor and agency head and their recommendations; and

3.    Stop the practice of transferring accountability from one AO to another whether bonded or not.

We recommended that management continue to exert earnest effort to ensure proper monitoring and implementation of the LDRRM PPAs within the targeted timelines in order to maximize the utilization of funds available.

We recommended that management include activities to be funded from the unexpended QRF and DRRMF-MOOE of the previous years which were transferred to the STF in the preparation of the LDRRMFIP which will be included in the AIP as required under COA Circular No. 2012-002. In the event that these activities were not initially included in the LDRRMFIP and AIP for the year, prepare and submit a revised LDRRMFIP as recommended for approval by the LDRRMC.

We acknowledged the diligent efforts performed by the LSB for the continuous improvement of utilization of SEF over the years however, we still recommended to maximize the utilization of funds through implementation of the activities and projects included in the SEF Budget to address the need for quality education. Further, for the Municipal Accountant and the OIC-Municipal Budget Officer to reconcile discrepancy noted in the reports they prepared and submitted.

We recommended that the LGU GFPS adopt a system that will ensure close monitoring of the execution of all PPAs to warrant their efficient and timely completion in order to achieve the desirable outcomes for the general welfare targeting gender issues and concerns.

We recommended that the GAD Focal Point System:

a)    Strictly comply with the provisions of Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA and other applicable rulings relative to the submission of the Annual GAD Accomplishment Reports and ensure that all planned projects and activities are implemented; and

b)    Maintain a monitoring system of all activities implemented and utilized funds to serve as the office’s record for preparing the GAD Accomplishment Report ahead of the deadline of submission.

We recommended that the management ensure that RFQs are duly filled up with complete essential information of prospective suppliers to promote transparency and efficiency during the procurement process.

We recommended that the management maintain adequate internal control on cash by providing the concerned accountable officers with individual safe or cash boxes where to keep their undeposited collections and unused accountable forms to ensure that such are properly separated and secured. Moreover, provide security escort during the transport of funds/cash by the depositing accountable officer to the bank.
We recommended that the LDRRMO submit the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of report within the prescribed period required under COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended that management direct the COs and LO to turn over their collections daily and intact to the LO and Municipal Treasurer, respectively, and follow strictly the rules and regulations on the collection, deposit and recording of funds to avoid risks of loss and/or misuse of funds in their custody.

We recommended that the management maintain adequate internal control on cash by providing the concerned accountable officers with individual safe or cash boxes where to keep their undeposited collections and unused accountable forms to ensure that such are properly separated and secured. Moreover, provide security escort during the transport of funds/cash by the depositing accountable officer to the bank.

We recommended that the management require and monitor the SDOs to record cash advances and disbursements daily in the cashbook, reconcile daily the cashbook balance with the cash on hand, foot and close the books and certify the balance thereof at the end of each month pursuant to the provisions of Section 28, Volume II of the Manual on New Government Accounting System for LGUs.
We recommended that the LDRRMO submit the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of report within the prescribed period required under COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended that management comply with the required staffing pattern taking into consideration its service requirements and financial capability, subject to the minimum standards and guidelines prescribed by the Civil Service Commission (CSC).

We recommended that management direct the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary correcting journal entries to reflect the correct balances of the appropriate accounts and to furnish the Auditor a copy of the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) taking up the adjustments.

We recommend that management direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to reconcile the actual amounts presented in the SCBAA and the actual amounts presented in the financial statements in compliance with Section 47 of the IPSAS No. 24 and present them in sufficient detail to enable the Municipality to better discharge its accountability obligations.

We recommended the following to management:

Designate separate employee for collection and disbursement functions to minimize the risk of misappropriation, lapping and juggling of funds. Those to be designated as Disbursing Officers must be appropriately bonded as such;

Refrain from transferring cash advances from one accountable officer to another. Designated disbursing officers perform actual payments to protect government funds from irregularities and non-liquidation of cash advances; and

Demand the responsible accountable officers to immediately liquidate their outstanding cash advances to avoid withholding of payment of any money due to them as provided for by COA Circular No. 97-002.

We recommend that management direct the OIC-Municipal Treasurer and Designated LO to deposit intact all collections and refrain from transferring or lending their collections to other officials to protect government funds from misappropriations and to protect themselves from being subjects of administrative and/or criminal charges.

We recommended that the management ensure the adequate and proper bonding of the AOs concerned and immediately apply for fidelity bond to cover their cash accountabilities as required under Treasury Circular No. 02-2019 to protect the interest of the government.

We recommended that management direct the concerned AOs to:

Reflect the corrections and record the unrecorded collections and turn over after the certification made by the Auditor in the cashbook; and

Properly record all collections and deposits made in the Cashbook or equivalent and update daily the cashbook for easy verification, monitoring and reconciliation of cash balances with accounting and related records.

We recommended that the collecting officer prepare the monthly RAAF to properly and correctly record all accountabilities for accountable forms as of a given period for easy verification, monitoring and reconciliation.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant to carefully review the supporting documents pertaining to claims for transportation and travelling expenses incurred by COS and JO personnel and ensure that such are supported with the additional requirements prescribed in Item B.5 of COA Resolution No. 2021-044.

We recommended that the LGU establish a temporary GAD database that is manually operated while waiting for the software from DILG. The LGU may utilize its existing database, planning and data-gathering tools if any to generate gender statistics, age and sex-disaggregated data and other GAD-related information from primary and secondary sources of data such as surveys, studies, focus group discussion (FGD) results, progress reports, monitoring and evaluation results and ODA reports. This will serve as source of information for the conduct of gender analysis, identify gender issues and determine gender responsive PPAs and be able to incorporate them in the GAD Plan and Budget for costing.

We recommended that the GAD Focal Point System assist the formulation and passage of GAD codes or ordinances to support the LGU in promoting, protecting and fulfilling women’s human rights, women economic empowerment and gender-responsive governance towards the attainment of gender equality and women’s empowerment.
We recommended that the LCE issue an Executive Order for the creation of GAD M&E team that would help enhance and maintain a gender monitoring and evaluation system.

We recommend that the LCE direct the MBO and the MPDC to:

Reconcile the 2022 Annual Budget and the AIP on 20 percent DF and establish their correctness, revise if erroneous and submit to the audit team the corrected documents; and

Ensure that the PPAs and appropriations in the Annual Budget is consistent with the Annual Investment Plan as approved and adopted by the MDC.

We recommended that management ensure that brand names, or if no brand state “no brand”, be indicated specifically for each item procured in the Purchase Orders, in compliance with COA Circular No. 96-010.

We recommend that the management direct its GSO to:

Retrieve the records of the properties found to have no tags and facilitate the placement/attachment of such Property Tags/Stickers on all properties for easier cross-referencing with the AREs and proper identification of the properties; and,

Attach Property Tags to the newly acquired PPEs before these are distributed to the intended end-users to establish control on the use of the government property and equipment.

We recommended and Management required the Municipal Engineer to ensure that all PPAs are provided with the prescribed tarpaulin signboards with the project status in order to promote good governance and secure the right of the people to information on matters of public concern.

We recommended the following:

The Municipal Treasurer and the MEEDO provide the Municipal Accountant with the List of Delinquent Market Stallholders with amount due and penalties to facilitate timely recording of accrued rental income; and

Demand payment from the delinquent market stallholders.

We recommended the following:

The Municipal Accountant make the necessary adjusting entries in the books to reflect the correct balances of the affected accounts; and

The Municipal Accountant and the General Services Officer maintain Supplies Ledger Cards for each commodity or stock, and periodically reconcile their balances to determine at any given time the details, status and cost of the inventory as prescribed.

We recommended that management post the Monthly Report on Fund Utilization and Status of Program/Project/Activity Implementation in three conspicuous places such as Municipal Hall where the Bulletin Board is located and send a written notice to the DBM, House of Representatives, Senate of the Philippines, House Committee on Appropriations, Senate Committee on Finance, as required in LBC No. 125.

1.6 We recommended and management agreed to continue all the efforts and activities that contribute to the positive increase of revenue most particularly those affecting income from local sources and find ways to improve those activities that do not yield positive results.

4.9 We recommended and management agreed to:
4.9.1 Direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to ensure that the actual expenses for Personal Services for one fiscal year shall not exceed its annual appropriations and the required 45% PS limitation. Henceforth, strictly incur expenses within the approved appropriation/budget, otherwise the excess would be disallowed in audit;
4.9.2 Exert effort in reducing its PS expenses by identifying and funding only PS items that are of priority concerns so that it can continue to allocate adequate funds for the implementation of other planned and development projects that are beneficial to its constituents; and
4.9.3 Exert effort in raising revenues to fund its additional PS costs.

5.3 We recommended and management agreed to the establishment of specific set of guidelines in the granting of financial assistance to its constituents to ensure efficient implementation of the program and fund utilization. The guidelines may include, among others, the following:
a) Qualifications of the recipients which may be determined by the MSWDO to include class and income levels.
b) Category of ailments, in the case of medical assistance, such as ordinary, medical or surgical cases.
c) Maximum amount of financial assistance for each category.
d) Maximum number of days to process and release the financial assistance.
e) Frequency or maximum number of entitlements in a year.

6.5 We recommended and management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary journal entries to reflect the correct classification of liability accounts. Henceforth, analysis should be made on the nature of the transaction for proper recording and accounting in the books of accounts.
7.5 We recommended that Management require the Accounting Office to record expenses in their proper classification in accordance to COA Circular No. 2015-009.

8.5 We recommended that management require:
8.5.1 The Accounting Office to record first the purchases of drugs and medicines, medical supplies, personal protective equipment, and other goods in the appropriate inventory account/s, and recognize the corresponding expenses for actual issuances to the units; and
8.5.2 The GSO to take control and custody of the drugs and medicines, medical supplies and personal protective equipment, strictly follow the procedures in the requisition and issuance of inventory items, and maintain stock cards.

9.6 We recommended that management adopt the use of ATM payroll system for all of its employees and require them to open an ATM payroll account with Land Bank of the Philippines to facilitate the crediting of salaries, wages and allowances to their account.

10.3 We recommended that management adopt policies and internal controls to monitor loan accommodations and other payroll deductions such that the monthly net take home pay of any employee does not fall below the prescribed ceiling of P5,000.00 as provided under Section 49 of RA 11518.

11.7 We recommended that management:
11.7.1 Require the Municipal Accountant and/or HRMO to submit to the Audit Team the lacking documents in order to effect proper review of TLB payments;
11.7.2 Direct the Municipal Accountant and OIC-Municipal Treasurer to submit justifications to the Auditor’s Offices why retired employees were cleared despite having unsettled accountabilities; and
11.7.3 Ensure that all accountabilities of retiring employees be settled first before Clearance Forms for Terminal Leave Benefits are signed.

12.6 We recommended that the management review attached supporting documents such as DTRs and Accomplishment Reports and strictly grant Hazard Pay to qualified employees only during periods of actual exposure to hazards and hardships in accordance to item 5.0 of DBM-DSWD Joint Circular No.1, series of 2018.

13.4 We have recommended and management agreed to strictly comply with Section 18 of the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 that specification for the procurement of goods shall be based on relevant characteristics and/or performance requirements and that reference to brand names shall not be allowed.

14.6 We recommended that management ensure that brand names and complete detailed item specifications of the items procured are indicated in the Purchase Orders, in compliance with COA Circular No. 96-010.

15.7 We recommended that Officers responsible for the preparation of the ABCs and the BAC comply with the guidelines set forth in DPWH Department Order No. 197, s. 2016 in the preparation of the ABCs.

16.8 We recommended that management:
16.8.1 Direct the BAC to disqualify prospective suppliers whose authorized line of business is outside the nature of what is being procured;
16.8.2 Ensure that the conditions for the SVP as an alternative mode of procurement have been satisfied, otherwise, conduct public bidding, or other appropriate alternative mode of procurement in accordance with R.A. No. 9184; and
16.8.3 Refrain from awarding contracts to those who are not technically, legally and financially capable as prescribed.

17.4 We recommended that management direct the Municipal Accountant to deduct the retention money from every progress payment or require the contractors to place standby letters of credit, bank guarantees and/or surety bonds before progress payments are made in strict adherence to Annex E, Paragraph 6 of the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184.

18.5 We recommended and management agreed to seek the required prior approval of the Sangguniang Bayan in relation to the implementation of projects charged against lump sum appropriations which are described in generic terms otherwise the projects/programs/activities in the AIP must be described with particularity to facilitate implementation.

19.4 We recommended and management agreed to enter into MOA with each of the beneficiary barangays regarding the implementation of development projects funded out of the Counterpart Fund. The MOA must provide clear terms and conditions, ownership of completed projects and sustainability of the projects.

20.7 We recommended and management agreed that the Local Development Council adopt a system that will ensure close monitoring of the execution of all PPAs to warrant their efficient and timely completion in order to deliver the desirable socio-economic and environmental development due to the public. Furthermore, the Municipal Engineer coordinate with the agencies concerned to fast track the implementation of the projects.
21.4 We recommended and management agreed to prepare the LDRRMFIP for the DRRM program annually in the prescribed format required under COA Circular No. 2012-002.

22.7 We recommended to management the following:
22.7.1 Consider webinars, video trainings, and the likes as alternative to face-to-face trainings or limit the number of participants to a minimum and observe proper health protocol, if physical training cannot be dispensed with, to continually enhance the LGU personnel’s knowledge and capabilities in relation to disaster preparedness and response; and
22.7.2 Direct the LDRRM Officer to monitor the implementation of the LDRRM PPAs to ensure that the public receive the benefits that may accrue from the utilization of the fund within the targeted timelines.

23.8 We recommended that the LGU establish GAD database to contain sex disaggregated data to serve as source of information for the conduct of gender analysis, identify gender issues and determine gender responsive PPAs and incorporate them in the GAD Plan and Budget for costing.

24.5 We recommended that management direct the GFPS to prepare and submit for DILG endorsement any amended/supplemental GPB in case of approved supplemental budgets and update the GAD budget attributed cost based on the final appropriation at year-end to determine the actual percentage of compliance with GAD budget requirement.

25.8 We recommended that the Local School Board maximize the utilization of the SEF through implementation of the programs, activities and projects included in the SEF Budget and incorporate allowable expenses to support the BE-LCP as provided in DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 2, s. 2020. Further for both the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to reconcile discrepancies noted in the reports they prepared and submitted.

26.10 We recommended and management agreed:
26.10.1 Conduct intensive Information, Education and Communication (IEC) campaign on proper segregation of wastes at source through fora, focus group discussions and trainings/workshops to apprise the people on strict implementation of the mandatory segregation of solid wastes policies;
26.10.2 Involve closely the barangays and its constituents in the implementation of the strategies of the LGU’s MSWMP as the barangay will be made responsible for the collection of segregated recyclable and compostable materials within its jurisdiction. Every barangay, therefore, is to collect segregated wastes from the households to be stored at the barangay MRF; and
26.10.3 Firmly enforce the existing Municipal Ordinance No. 99-2011 including its penal provisions in order to support the Mandatory Segregation of Solid Waste policy of RA 9003 to achieve minimization and reduction of wastes.

27.4 We recommended and management agreed to provide new personal protective equipment to all collectors and personnel directly dealing with solid waste and require the concerned personnel to wear them at all times during work to protect them from the hazard of handling waste.

We recommended to management that all major repairs and improvements of assets that extend the life, increase the capacity or improve the efficiency or safety of the property be capitalized and for the accounting unit to make the necessary adjusting entries to recognize/derecognize the related asset/expense account where applicable in the CY 2020 financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant not to recognize payables pending deliveries or performance of services by suppliers and adjust correspondingly the LGU’s recorded payables, assets and expenses for the calendar year 2020 financial statements.
We recommended that management strictly adhere to the policies governing the grant of PBB so as not to irregularly spend our scarce and limited public funds.

As fiscal responsibility shall be shared by all those exercising authority over the financial affairs, transactions and operations of the LGU, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive, the local Sanggunian and other Officials concerned ensure that sound and balance budget be attained, and that members of the Local Finance Committee exercise its mandatory functions as provided for in Sec. 316 of RA No. 7160.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant cause the deduction and withholding of the 1% retention money from the payment of goods procured or require a special bank guarantee from suppliers which is equivalent to 1% of the total contract price. Said amounts shall only be released after the lapse of the warranty period of one year or after consumption whichever is applicable.

We recommended that the Local Development Council review the programs and projects funded by the 20% Economic Development Fund and incorporate only those allowable projects in the Annual Investment Plan.

We recommended that management submit the lacking supporting documents on the SEF disbursements. Further for the Municipal Accountant to strictly check completeness of documentary requirements to support payments of salaries and wages before certifying on the disbursement voucher.

We recommended that management to direct the Municipal Accountant and MDRRMO to submit Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF in the prescribed format on or before the 15th day after the end of each month.

We recommended that management conduct the semestral inventory taking and submission of the Report on the Physical Count of Inventories not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second semesters, respectively, for verification and review.

As fiscal responsibility shall be shared by all those exercising authority over the financial affairs, transactions and operations of the LGU, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive, the local Sanggunian and other Officials concerned to ensure that sound and balance budget be attained, and that members of the Local Finance Committee exercise its mandatory functions as provided for in Sec. 316 of RA No. 7160.

The Municipal Accountant recognize the unpaid rentals and its penalties, if applicable, in the books of the Municipality as receivable and income accounts, in compliance with the provisions of IPSAS 13; and
Demand payment from the delinquent market stallholders.

9.5 We recommended that the Local Development Council review the programs and projects funded by the 20% Economic Development Fund and incorporate only those allowable projects in the Annual Investment Plan.

10.4 We recommended that management submit the lacking supporting documents on the SEF disbursements. Further for the Municipal Accountant to strictly check completeness of documentary requirements to support payments of salaries and wages before certifying on the disbursement voucher.

11.5 We recommended that management:
a.	Prepare LDRRMFIP for the DRRM program annually in the prescribed format and submit it as required under COA Circular No. 2012-002; and
b.	Direct the Municipal Accountant and MDRRMO to submit Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF in the prescribed format on or before the 15th day after the end of each month.
13.3 We recommended that the Local School Board maximize the utilization of the SEF through implementation of the programs, activities and projects included in the SEF Budget.

We recommended that Management direct the Accountant to review the composition of outstanding checks and record the adjusting entries to restore the amount of stale checks to the “Cash in Bank” account and other appropriate accounts at year-end.

We recommended that management immediately send demand letters to all the delinquent stall and block holders and apply to the remedies by administrative or judicial action as enumerated in Section 174 of RA 7160 if payments for rent were still not recovered.

We recommended that the LGU’s accounting unit reconcile the discrepancies noted and to make the necessary adjustments/corrections, as necessary, in the books of accounts to reflect the correct balances of the Due from Other Funds, Due to Other Funds, Due from Special Accounts and Due To Special Accounts.
We recommended that management reflect the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF in the Notes to Financial Statements in compliance to COA Circular No. 2012-12.

We recommended that:

The Municipal Treasurer observe strictly Section 321 of RA No. 7160 in the preparation of supplemental budget, only certifying that funds are actually available.

We recommended that:

The Local Finance Committee prudently identify fund sources for appropriation ordinances and consider the feasibility of their collections; and determine doable measures that can uplift collection efficiency of these fund sources.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant look into the Disbursement Voucher and Obligation Request if it is properly and completely accomplished before a particular transaction is processed. Further, for the Budget Officer to properly and completely accomplish the Obligation Request in accordance with Section 2.0 of COA Circular No. 2006-002 dated January 31, 2006.

We recommended that Management:

Prepare LDRRMFIP for the DRRM program annually in the prescribed format required under COA Circular No. 2012-002; and

Direct the Municipal Accountant and MDRRMO to submit Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF in prescribed format on or before the 15th day after the end of each month.

We recommended that:

observations.reason: BRS was not submitted within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to Management the following:  1.) refrain from granting additional cash advances to Accountable Officer with previous unliquidated cash advances; 2.)   enforce full liquidation of cash advances within the reglementary period provided under COA Circular No.97-002; 3.)  demand the concerned officials and employees with unliquidated cash advances to effect immediate settlement of cash advances, otherwise, the necessary sanction for its settlement as provided for in paragraph 9.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-004 shall be enforced; and 4.) comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2016-005 to file for request of write-off on dormant accounts.
observations.reason: Some of the accountable officers have already settled their accountabilities.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to Management the following:  1.) stop the practice of granting additional cash advances to the concern Accountable Officer without settlement or proper accounting thereof of the previous cash advances to prevent possible misuse or misappropriation of funds; and 2.) when granting wages for laborers on certain various undertakings on a specified month, attached the various payrolls on the identified undertakings and grant only a single cash advance for the said payrolls.

We recommended to Management the following:  1.) submit the cash advance of the CF with complete supporting documents as enumerated in the Joint Circular;  2.) state clearly in the Disbursement Voucher (DV) the duration of the implementation of the project/activity; and 3.) submit all DVs for cash advances with complete supporting documents within (7) working days from release of the check to the COA ATL concerned.

We recommended to Management the following:  1.) strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 to facilitate the review and evaluation process and attention is also invited to the penalty clause provision of said Circular; and 2.) submit copies of the contract and its integral documents enumerated in Appendix A for contract review.
observations.reason: Contracts still not submitted within the required period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to Management the following: 1.) The Mahayag Government Employees’ Association (MAGEA) to have their Association be registered with the Civil Service Commission being the sole and exclusive negotiating agent for the purpose of collective negotiation with management; and 2.) The sources of funds from MOOE should be clearly indicated that said savings were sourced from savings from travelling expenses; communication expenses; repairs and maintenance; transportation and delivery expenses; supplies and Materials and Utility Expenses.
observations.reason: The sources of funds from MOOE did not clearly indicated that said savings were sourced from savings from travelling expenses; communication expenses; repairs and maintenance; transportation and delivery expenses; supplies and Materials and Utility Expenses.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to Management the following:  1.) stop from charging food expenses for office consumption against the LGU’s appropriations; and 2.) food stuff expenses be charged against the Representation Allowance granted the Municipal Mayor and the Sangguniang Bayan members.

We recommended to Management the following:  1.) implement the salary increase to all Municipal Officials and Employees under the Second Tranche Compensation Adjustment for Local Government Personnel effective January 1, 2017, based on the salary grade and step indicated under the third class municipality; 2.) refrain from implementing the Third Tranche Compensation Adjustment for CY 2018 and succeeding years until the Salary Rates are commensurate to the Income Classification of the Municipality; and 3.) require the employees concerned to refund the excess amount of salary increase collected.
observations.reason: No salary adjustment had been effected.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management:  1.) practice the conduct of public bidding in the procurement process in order to ensure the most advantageous price and generate income from sale of bid documents; 2.) include in the timeline of the competitive bidding procurement process the goods/supplies to be used on the recurrent activities as well as predetermined activities during the year; 3.) require the BAC to evaluate the frequency of Small Value Procurement (SVP)being resorted and to ensure that the conditions for the SVP as an alternative mode of procurement have been satisfied; 4.) formulate inventory turnover system on the procurement of spare parts in order to determine the re-order point; 5.) comply with the documentary requirements for completeness and propriety of the transactions; and 6.) stop the practice of paying purchases thru reimbursement basis.  Payment be made directly to suppliers of the goods and services.

We recommended to Management the following:  1.) require the BAC to evaluate the frequency of SVP being resorted and to ensure that the conditions for SVP as an alternative mode of procurement have been satisfied; 2.) formulate inventory turnover system on the procurement of office supplies, medicines,  medical supplies and equipment in order to determine the re-order point; 3.) refrain from splitting the procurement and consolidate requisitions for one or more items needed at or about the same time by the requisitioner or Purchase Order (PO)  based on two(2) or more requisitions for the same or about the same time by different requisitioners; and 5.) practice the conduct of public bidding in the procurement process in order to ensure the most advantageous price and generate income from sale of bid documents.

We recommended to Management the following:  1.) ensure that the procurement of common-use supplies be directly procured from the PS-DBM in compliance with RA 9184 and Administrative Order No. 17; 2.) secure a certification from the PS-DBM for items that are not available; and 3.) in case the ordinary or regular office supplies and equipment are not available in the PS-DBM, the procuring entity may obtain the items through the alternative mode of procurement known as shopping under Section 52 (b) of the IRR-A of R.A No. 1984.

We recommended that the TWG exercise utmost prudence in the post-qualification of the bids in order to properly determine whether the bidder concerned complies with and are responsive to all the requirements and conditions specified in the IRR and provide evaluation data on the post-qualification of the bidders such as:  1.) examination of expiration date of the PhilGEPS Registration at the time of bid opening; 2.) ascertain that the bidder should not have any tax liability with the Government from the time of submission of its bid up to the time of contract award; 3.) the bidder’s Audited Financial Statement be stamped “Received” by the BIR; and 4.) bid Security in the prescribed form be included as part of the bidding documents in the first envelope pursuant to Section 25.2.a.vi of the IRR.

We recommended to management the following:  1.) The Local Development Council monitor the implementation of the projects as incorporated in the Annual Investment Plan; 2.)  The Municipal Engineer coordinate with the departments concerned to fast track the implementation of the projects; 3.)  The Municipal Mayor shall ensure compliance with the responsibilities provided for in paragraph 5.0 of DILG and DBM Joint Circular No. 2017-1 to achieve desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes; and 4.)  Incorporate projects in the Annual Invest Plan that is within the scope of allowable as identified in social development, economic development and environmental management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the management the following:  1.) strictly follow the JC No. 1, s. 2017 of the DepEd, DBM and DILG in the preparation and presentation of SEF budget using SEF Budget Preparation Form No. 1 (Annex A) and based it on the DepEd-approved SIP and DEDP in the implementation of ECCD Program, kindergarten, elementary and secondary, formal and non-formal education programs; 2.) post the utilization report of the SEF Budget in the website of the LGU and/or in at least three (3) conspicuous public places for transparency and accountability in compliance with the Full Disclosure Policy of the DILG; and 3.) furnish COA with copies of the Quarterly and Annual Reports of SEF Utilization, as prepared by the Local Accountant, received by DepEd, the local sanggunian concerned and Regional Offices of the DBM and DILG.

We recommended to Management the following:  1.) use NDRRMC, DBM and DILG Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2013-1 as a guide in the formulation of the LDRRMFIP programs/projects/activities using  the four (4) thematic areas as mandated in R.A. No. 10121; and 2.) review and update regularly the LDRRMFIP to consider current vulnerabilities that may arise.
observations.reason: Some of the unimplemented projects were being implemented in CY 2018.

observations.recommendation: In view of the foregoing observations noted above, we recommended that the GAD Focal Point System:  1.) strictly comply with the provisions of Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA and other applicable rulings relative to the submission of the approved GAD Plan and Budget and the preparation of the Annual GAD Accomplishment Reports to be approved by the different offices concerned and expenditures should be limited to the amount approved in the GAD Plan and Budget to ensure that all planned projects and activities are implemented; 2.) attend GAD related seminars and trainings to keep abreast with updates and build up competencies in the execution of GAD related laws and regulations; 3.) gather and build up sex-disaggregated data and GAD related information that can be sourced in the agencies suggested by the Circular such as the NSO, NSCB, DSWD and DepED; Maintain and establish GAD database to serve as basis for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation; and 4.) assist formulation and passage of GAD codes or ordinances to support the LGU in promoting, protecting and fulfilling women’s human rights, women economic empowerment and gender-responsive governance towards the attainment of gender equality and women’s empowerment.

      We recommended to Management the following:  1.) implement the  salary
increase to all Municipal Officials and Employees under the  Second  Tranche
Compensation Adjustment for Local Government Personnel effective January  1,
2017, based on the salary grade and step indicated  under  the  third  class
municipality; 2.) refrain from implementing the Third  Tranche  Compensation
Adjustment for CY 2018 and succeeding  years  until  the  Salary  Rates  are
commensurate to the Income  Classification  of  the  Municipality;  and  3.)
require the employees concerned  to  refund  the  excess  amount  of  salary
increase collected.

Management’s Comment:

      Management assured that they will comply with the recommendations.

Recommendation:

      We recommended that Management:  1.) practice the  conduct  of  public
bidding in the procurement process in order to ensure the most  advantageous
price and generate income from sale of bid documents;  2.)  include  in  the
timeline of the competitive bidding procurement process  the  goods/supplies
to be used on the recurrent activities as well as  predetermined  activities
during the year; 3.) require the BAC to  evaluate  the  frequency  of  Small
Value Procurement (SVP)being resorted and to ensure that the conditions  for
the SVP as an alternative mode  of  procurement  have  been  satisfied;  4.)
formulate inventory turnover system on the procurement  of  spare  parts  in
order to determine the re-order  point;  5.)  comply  with  the  documentary
requirements for completeness and propriety of  the  transactions;  and  6.)
stop the practice of paying purchases thru reimbursement basis.  Payment  be
made directly to suppliers of the goods and services.

Recommendation:

       We recommended to Management the following:  1.) require the  BAC  to
evaluate the frequency  of  SVP  being  resorted  and  to  ensure  that  the
conditions  for  SVP  as  an  alternative  mode  of  procurement  have  been
satisfied; 2.) formulate inventory turnover system  on  the  procurement  of
office supplies, medicines,  medical supplies  and  equipment  in  order  to
determine the re-order point; 3.) refrain  from  splitting  the  procurement
and consolidate requisitions for one or more items needed at  or  about  the
same time by the requisitioner or Purchase Order (PO)  based  on  two(2)  or
more requisitions  for  the  same  or  about  the  same  time  by  different
requisitioners; and 5.) practice  the  conduct  of  public  bidding  in  the
procurement process in order to  ensure  the  most  advantageous  price  and
generate income from sale of bid documents.

Recommendation:

      We recommended to Management  the  following:   1.)  ensure  that  the
procurement of common-use supplies be directly procured from the  PS-DBM  in
compliance with RA 9184 and  Administrative  Order  No.  17;  2.)  secure  a
certification from the PS-DBM for items that are not available; and  3.)  in
case  the  ordinary  or  regular  office  supplies  and  equipment  are  not
available in the PS-DBM, the procuring entity may obtain the  items  through
the alternative mode of procurement known as shopping under Section  52  (b)
of the IRR-A of R.A No. 1984.

Recommendation:

             We recommended that the TWG exercise  utmost  prudence  in  the
post-qualification of the bids in order to properly  determine  whether  the
bidder concerned complies with and are responsive to  all  the  requirements
and conditions specified in the IRR and provide evaluation data on the post-
qualification of the bidders such as:  1.) examination  of  expiration  date
of the PhilGEPS Registration at the time of bid opening; 2.) ascertain  that
the bidder should not have any tax liability with the  Government  from  the
time of submission of its bid up to the time  of  contract  award;  3.)  the
bidder’s Audited Financial Statement be stamped “Received” by the  BIR;  and
4.) bid Security in the prescribed form be included as part of  the  bidding
documents in the first envelope pursuant to Section 25.2.a.vi of the IRR.

Recommendation:

      We recommended to management the following:  1.) the Rural Health Unit
(RHU) and Mahayag Municipal Health (MMH) when procuring  medicines  indicate
the appropriate data information in detail as required in COA  Circular  No.
96-010 dated August 15, 1996; 2.) use the Drug Price Index Reference  (DRPI)
as guide for the ceiling prices of medicines to be procured;   and  3.)  the
winning supplier indicate the date of receipt under  the  conforme  part  of
the Purchase Order as reckoning date to compute the  liquidated  damages  in
case there is  failure to deliver the goods as agreed upon.

Recommendation:

      We recommended to management the following:  1.) The Local Development
Council monitor the implementation of the projects as  incorporated  in  the
Annual Investment Plan; 2.)  The  Municipal  Engineer  coordinate  with  the
departments concerned to fast track the implementation of the projects;  3.)
 The Municipal Mayor  shall  ensure  compliance  with  the  responsibilities
provided for in paragraph 5.0 of DILG and DBM Joint Circular No.  2017-1  to
achieve desirable socio-economic  development  and  environmental  outcomes;
and 4.)  Incorporate projects in the Annual Invest Plan that is  within  the
scope  of  allowable  as  identified   in   social   development,   economic
development and environmental management.

Recommendation:

      We recommended to the management the following:  1.)  strictly  follow
the JC No. 1, s. 2017 of the DepEd, DBM and  DILG  in  the  preparation  and
presentation of SEF budget using SEF Budget Preparation Form  No.  1  (Annex
A) and based it on the DepEd-approved SIP and DEDP in the implementation  of
ECCD Program, kindergarten, elementary and secondary, formal and  non-formal
education programs; 2.) post the utilization report of  the  SEF  Budget  in
the website of the LGU and/or in  at  least  three  (3)  conspicuous  public
places for transparency and  accountability  in  compliance  with  the  Full
Disclosure Policy of the DILG; and  3.)  furnish  COA  with  copies  of  the
Quarterly and Annual Reports of SEF Utilization, as prepared  by  the  Local
Accountant, received by DepEd, the local sanggunian concerned  and  Regional
Offices of the DBM and DILG.

Recommendation:

      We recommended that Management prepare and submit the  Monthly  Report
on Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (Annex B)  for
evaluation and verification within the prescribed period set.

Recommendation:

      We recommended to Management the following:  1.) use NDRRMC,  DBM  and
DILG  Joint  Memorandum  Circular  (JMC)  No.  2013-1  as  a  guide  in  the
formulation of the LDRRMFIP  programs/projects/activities  using   the  four
(4) thematic areas as mandated in R.A. No. 10121; and 2.) review and  update
regularly the LDRRMFIP to consider current vulnerabilities that may arise.

Recommendation:

      In view of the foregoing  observations  noted  above,  we  recommended
that the GAD Focal Point System:  1.) strictly comply  with  the  provisions
of Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA and  other
applicable rulings relative to the submission of the approved GAD  Plan  and
Budget and the preparation of the Annual GAD Accomplishment  Reports  to  be
approved by the different  offices  concerned  and expenditures  should  be
limited to the amount approved in the GAD Plan and  Budget  to  ensure  that
all planned projects and activities are implemented; 2.) attend GAD  related
seminars  and  trainings  to  keep  abreast  with  updates  and   build   up
competencies in the execution of  GAD  related  laws  and  regulations;  3.)
gather and build up sex-disaggregated data and GAD related information  that
can be sourced in the agencies suggested by the Circular such  as  the  NSO,
NSCB, DSWD and DepED; Maintain and establish GAD database to serve as  basis
for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation; and  4.)
assist formulation and passage of GAD codes or  ordinances  to  support  the
LGU in promoting, protecting and  fulfilling  women’s  human  rights,  women
economic  empowerment   and   gender-responsive   governance   towards   the
attainment of gender equality and women’s empowerment.

Management’s Comment:

      Management assured that they will comply with the recommendations.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:

      We recommend that management:  1.) refrain  from  granting  additional
cash  advances  to  Accountable  Officer  with  previous  unliquidated  cash
advances; 2.)  prepare supporting  schedule  of  Advances  to  Officers  and
Employees account to identify the accountable officers concerned  and  Aging
of Cash Advances Schedule; 3.) enforce full  liquidation  of  cash  advances
within the reglementary period provided under COA Circular  No.97-002  dated
February 10, 1997; and 4.)  demand the  concerned  officials  and  employees
with unliquidated cash advances  to  effect  immediate  settlement  of  cash
advances, otherwise, the necessary sanction for its settlement  as  provided
for in paragraph 9.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-004 dated  November  28,  2012
shall be enforced.

Recommendation:

      We recommend the following, to wit:  1.)The  Municipal  Accountant  to
book up as Expenses all obligations incurred during the year as required  in
Section 119 of P.D. 1445; 2.) At the end of the  year  and  in  cases  where
payment cannot be effected yet, said  obligations  shall  be  booked  up  as
Expense and the corresponding Accounts Payable be set up  at  the  year  the
expenses were incurred;  and 3.)  The Municipal  Budget  Officer  to  adopt
appropriate measures to control the charges made  under  the  appropriations
of programs, projects and activities to which it was created and  to  ensure
that all obligations  are  duly  backed  up  with  cash  and  not  only  the
availability of appropriations but the cash availability as well.

Recommendation:

      We recommend that management:  1.) Create an  inventory  committee  to
conduct a  physical  count  of  the  properties  of  the  municipality;  2.)
Maintain a Property, Plant and Equipment Register to facilitate the  conduct
of year-end physical inventory, the municipality  use this as basis  in  the
conduct and reconciliation of the accounts; and 3.) Submit  a  copy  of  the
RPCPPE to the Office of the Auditor not later  than  January  31st  of  each
year as provided in Volume  II  of  the  New  Government  Accounting  System
(NGAS) to facilitate verification of the inventory report.

Recommendation:

      We recommend that:  1.) Officials involved in the processing of claims
review  thoroughly  the  completeness   of   supporting   documents   and/or
requirements for every transaction to ensure compliance with existing  rules
and regulations; and 2.) Management refrain from paying the claims if  these
are not supported with the  required  documents  and  information  to  avoid
suspending the transaction in audit.

Recommendation:

      We recommend that management strictly comply with  the  provisions  of
COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 to facilitate  the  review
and evaluation process and attention is also invited to the  penalty  clause
provision of said Circular.

Recommendation:

      We recommend that management insure its properties from damage to,  or
      loss of, due to fire, earthquake,  storm,  or  other  casualty  through
      the Government Service Insurance System as  mandated  under  R.A.  No.
      656,  as amended.

Recommendation:

      We recommend that the Accountant make the necessary correcting journal
entries to  reflect  the  correct  balances  of  the  appropriate  accounts.
Further, refrain from recognizing liabilities before goods and services  are
accepted or rendered and supplier/creditor bills are received.

Recommendation:

             We  recommend  that  the  Local  Executive  and  the  Municipal
Officials to comply  with  the  limitation  in  the  grant  of  the  PEI  to
P5,000.00 each  employee  pursuant  to  DBM  Budget  Circular  No.  2016-08.
Require the Municipal Officials and Employees to refund  the  excess  amount
of PEI collected in excess of the P5,000.00.

Recommendation:

      We recommended that: 1) The Municipal Accountant,  strictly  implement
the rates of RATA prescribed under Annex B of DBM LBC No.103 dated  May  15,
2013 under the 3rd class municipality and immediately stop  the  payment  of
excess RATA; 2)Scrutinize the supporting documents attached to  the  payroll
and immediately deduct the  amount  corresponding  to  the  absences  during
regular sessions before payment shall be made.

Recommendation:

      We recommend the Municipal Accountant to comply with Rule XVI  of  the
Omnibus Rules Implementing Book V of EO No. 292 as amended by  CSC  MC  Nos.
41 s. 1998, 6 s. 1999,  wherein payment of Terminal Leave Benefits  be  made
to officials and employees upon separation/resignation  or  retirement  from
the government service.

Recommendation:

      We recommend the following :  1.) Enact a Sangguniang Bayan Resolution
to set eligibility  criteria  for  qualified   recipients/beneficiaries  and
corresponding amounts for each class of  financial  assistance;  2.)  Ensure
that  each  case  be  properly  and  objectively  assessed  and   evaluated.
Financial assistance be granted  to  qualified/eligible  beneficiaries;  3.)
Completeness of documentary  requirements  be  complied  before  payment  is
made.

Recommendation:

      We recommend the following: 1.) require the  Municipal  Accountant  to
implement the salary increase  to  all  Municipal  Officials  and  Employees
under  the  First  Tranche  Compensation  Adjustment  for  Local  Government
Personnel effective January 1, 2016, based on  the  salary  grade  and  step
indicated under the third class municipality;  and  2.)  refund  the  excess
amount  of  salary  increase  collected  and  also  require  the   employees
concerned who claimed salary differential but not entitled  to  said  salary
increase since their salaries as of December 31, 2015 were higher  than  the
rates indicated under the First Tranche Compensation  Adjustment  for  Local
Government Personnel effective January 1, 2016.

Recommendation:

      We recommend that management:  1.) Require the  BAC  to  evaluate  the
frequency of Shopping being resorted and to ensure that the  conditions  for
the Shopping as an alternative mode of procurement have been satisfied;  2.)
Formulate inventory turnover system on the procurement  of  spare  parts  in
order to determine the re-order point; 3.) Practice the  conduct  of  public
bidding in the procurement process in order to ensure the most  advantageous
price and generate income from sale of bid documents; 4.)  Comply  with  the
documentary   requirements   for   completeness   and   propriety   of   the
transactions;  and  5.)  Stop  the  practice  of   paying   purchases   thru
reimbursement basis.  Payment be made directly to  suppliers  of  the  goods
and services.

Recommendation:

      We recommend that management:  1.) Require the  BAC  to  evaluate  the
frequency of SVP being resorted and to ensure that the  conditions  for  SVP
as an alternative mode of procurement have  been  satisfied;  2.)  Formulate
inventory turnover system on the procurement of office supplies,  medicines,
 medical supplies and equipment in order to determine  the  re-order  point;
3.) Refrain from splitting the procurement and consolidate requisitions  for
one or more items needed at or about the same time by the  requisitioner  or
Purchase Order (PO)  based on two(2) or more requisitions for  the  same  or
about the same time  by  different  requisitioners;  and  4.)  Practice  the
conduct of public bidding in the procurement process in order to ensure  the
most advantageous price and generate income from sale of bid documents.

Recommendation:

      We recommend that the TWG exert extra care in  the  post-qualification
of the bids in order to properly  determine  whether  the  bidder  concerned
complies with and are responsive to  all  the  requirements  and  conditions
specified in the IRR and provide evaluation data on  the  post-qualification
of the bidders.

Recommendation:

      We recommend that management: 1.) Require the BAC to  ensure  that
the conditions for SVP as an  alternative  mode  of  procurement  have  been
satisfied;  and  2.)  Practice  the  conduct  of  public  bidding   in   the
procurement process in order to  ensure  the  most  advantageous  price  and
generate income from the sale of bid documents.

Recommendation:

      We  recommend  that  management:  1.)  Adhere  to  the  Appendix   11,
Guidelines   on   the   Implementation   of   Infrastructure   Projects   by
Administration  in  the  Pakyaw  System  Contracting;  and  2.)  Submit  the
required  documents   enumerated   above   and/or   justification   of   the
deficiencies noted.

Recommendation:

      It is recommended that  management  impose  warranty  security  to  be
posted by the contractors on completed  infrastructure  projects  after  the
issuance  of  Certificate  of  Final  Acceptance  following  the  prescribed
schedule in Section 62.2.3.3. of the Revised IRR of R.A. 9184.

Recommendation:

      We recommend that:   1.)  Specification  on  relevant  characteristics
and/or performance  requirements  be  provided  as  well  as  the  necessary
information be filled-up in the purchase requests; and 2.)  Strictly  comply
the observance of non-reference to brand names.

Recommendation:

      We recommend that the guidelines  in  the  procurement  of  drugs  and
medicines as presented in the Revised Implementing Guidelines for  the  PNFS
be strictly followed and implemented.  Hence, drugs and medicines which  are
not in the list of the PNF manual be not included in  the  procurement.   If
the said items were proven effective and there is really a need  to  procure
the same, the concerned Municipal Health Officer make  a  request  following
the revised guidelines for the inclusion of the items in  the  upcoming  8th
edition of the PNF manual.

Recommendation:

      We recommend that the management:  1.) Enforce the liquidated  damages
for late delivery of items  procured;  and  2.)  Ensure  that  all  Purchase
Orders and Contracts  indicate  the  terms  and  date  of  delivery  with  a
provision on liquidated damages which shall be payable by the contractor  in
case of breach thereof as provided Section 68  of  the  Revised  IRR  of  RA
9184.

Recommendation:

          We recommended management to: 1.) Refrain from  using  the  Direct
Contracting  as  an  alternative  mode  of  procurement  unless   absolutely
necessary and that all conditions set forth by law  are  met  after  careful
and  thorough  evaluation,  otherwise  resort  only  to  competitive  public
bidding; and 2.) Practice the conduct of public bidding in  the  procurement
process in order to ensure the most advantageous price and  generate  income
from the sale of bid documents.

Recommendation:

      We recommend that persons responsible  in  the  issuance  of  Purchase
Orders for the municipality include all the pertinent data  as  required  in
COA Circular No. 96-010 dated August 15, 1996.

Recommendation:

      We recommend the following:  1.) The Local Development  Council  shall
monitor the implementation of the projects as  incorporated  in  the  Annual
Investment Plan; 2.)  The  Municipal  Engineer  shall  coordinate  with  the
departments concerned to fast track the implementation of the projects;  and
3.) The Municipal Mayor shall ensure compliance  with  the  responsibilities
provided for in paragraph 5.0 of DILG and DBM Joint Circular No.  2011-1  to
achieve desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes.

Recommendation:

      We recommended that  management  refrain  from  charging  expenditures
which are not covered in the Circular in order that optimal use of the  fund
will be for the priority projects, programs and activities set therein.

Recommendation:

      We recommend that in the preparation of the School Board  Budget,  the
members of the Local School Board give priority to programs, activities  and
projects set in Section 272 of the Local Government  Code  and  utilize  the
same to such purpose prescribed by law for which the SEF was created.

Recommendation:

      We recommend that management prepare and submit the Monthly Report  on
Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management  Fund  (Annex  B)  for
evaluation and verification.

Recommendation:

      In view of the foregoing observations noted above, we  recommend  that
the GAD Focal Point System:  1.) Strictly  comply  with  the  provisions  of
Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 of  the  PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA  and  other
applicable rulings relative to the submission of the approved GAD  Plan  and
Budget and the preparation of the Annual GAD Accomplishment  Reports  to  be
approved by the different  offices  concerned  and  expenditures  should  be
limited to the amount approved in the GAD Plan and  Budget  to  ensure  that
all planned projects and activities are implemented; 2.) Attend GAD  related
seminars  and  trainings  to  keep  abreast  with  updates  and   build   up
competencies in the execution of  GAD  related  laws  and  regulations;  3.)
Gather and build up sex-disaggregated data and GAD related information  that
can be sourced in the agencies  suggested  by  the  Circular;  Maintain  and
establish GAD database to serve as  basis  for  gender-responsive  planning,
programming and policy formulation; and 4.) Assist formulation  and  passage
of GAD codes or ordinances to support the LGU in promoting,  protecting  and
fulfilling women’s human rights,  women  economic  empowerment  and  gender-
responsive governance towards the attainment of gender equality and  women’s
empowerment.

Recommendation:

      In view of the foregoing observations noted above, we  recommend  that
the GAD Focal Point System:  1.) Strictly  comply  with  the  provisions  of
Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 of  the  PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA  and  other
applicable rulings relative to the submission of the approved GAD  Plan  and
Budget and the preparation of the Annual GAD Accomplishment  Reports  to  be
approved by the different  offices  concerned  and  expenditures  should  be
limited to the amount approved in the GAD Plan and  Budget  to  ensure  that
all planned projects and activities are implemented; 2.) Attend GAD  related
seminars  and  trainings  to  keep  abreast  with  updates  and   build   up
competencies in the execution of  GAD  related  laws  and  regulations;  3.)
Gather and build up sex-disaggregated data and GAD related information  that
can be sourced in the agencies  suggested  by  the  Circular;  Maintain  and
establish GAD database to serve as  basis  for  gender-responsive  planning,
programming and policy formulation; and 4.) Assist formulation  and  passage
of GAD codes or ordinances to support the LGU in promoting,  protecting  and
fulfilling women’s human rights,  women  economic  empowerment  and  gender-
responsive governance towards the attainment of gender equality and  women’s
empowerment.

Recommendation:

      It is recommended that management fully comply with the  role  of  the
LGU as provided for under the Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of  2000
to  have  a  systematic,  comprehensive  and  ecological  waste   management
program.

It is recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare monthly the Bank Reconciliation Statements and submit the same within the reglementary period prescribed under COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996 together with its supporting documents such as Monthly Bank Statement to facilitate the early submission of the BRS, Subsidiary Ledgers, paid checks, debit and credit memos and deposit slips; and prepare the necessary adjusting entries for any reconciling items noted in the process.

It is recommended that the Municipal Accountant: 1) prepare JEV to take up in the books of accounts the unrecorded CIP Account to correct the balance of the account; 2) exert effort to reconcile the balances and see to it that the CIP Account and advance payments, retention and the corresponding recoupment are properly accounted and recorded; and 3) review and analyze the account balances of previous years and prepare the proper adjustments, if warranted.

It is recommended that the Municipal Accountant, scrutinize the supporting documents attached to the payroll and immediately deduct the amount corresponding to the absences during regular sessions before payment shall be made and to strictly comply with the provisions set in DBM Local Budget Circular No. 103 dated May 15, 2013.

It is  recommended that the Municipal Accountant:  1) evaluate the claims for hazard pay allowance and subsistence allowance of the PHWs in conformity with the provisions of DBM and DOH Joint Circular No. 1, S. 2012;  2) the concerned PHWs  refund the excessive amount of hazard pay allowance and subsistence allowance claimed.

It is recommended that management require the Municipal Accountant to submit the reports and documents relative to the collections of and payments from government funds to enable the Auditor to conduct a timely verification and audit of the accounts of the Municipality otherwise the sanctions as provided in Section 122 of P.D. 1445 shall be imposed and submit justification for failure to submit the required reports and documents despite repeated demands.

The following are recommended: 1) The Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and the Municipal Development Council to monitor the implementation of the projects as incorporated in the Annual Investment Plan; 2)  The Municipal Engineer to coordinate with the departments concerned to fast track the implementation of the projects; and 3)  The Local Chief Executive to ensure compliance with the responsibilities provided for in COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013.

It is recommended that the BAC should comply with the pertinent provisions of R.A. 9184 specially during the preliminary examination of bids using the “pass/fail” criteria and during the post-qualification process whether the bidder concerned complies with and is responsive to all the requirements and conditions as specified in the Bidding Documents

It is recommended that the Bids and Award Committee (BAC) inform or furnish notices to the observers, in all stages of the procurement process to sit in its proceedings not only in pre-procurement conferences, pre-bid conferences and opening of bids.

It is recommended that management comply with the provisions of DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-73 dated April 17, 2012 in procurement of early warning systems, preparedness equipment and other equipage.

It is recommended that the Municipal Treasurer immediately remit the balance of the Due to BIR Account to avoid penalties, which are personal in nature, and help contribute to the collections for the operation of our national government.

The concerned personnel in the Municipal Engineer’s Office responsible for the preparation of the Program of Work shall include or incorporate the cost of billboard/s and SPL - construction safety and health in the preparation of the design, estimate and construction on the implementation of its infrastructure projects.  In addition, the installation of billboards on every infrastructure projects shall be monitored.
observations.managementAction: The LCE has ordered the Municipal Engineer to review all the POWs implemented by contract or by administration and see to it that pay item on billboards and health and safety measures be included in the POW.
observations.reason: The required reports were not submitted within the reglementary period.

observations.recommendation:
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to tap all available resources and exert all efforts to hasten and facilitate the preparation and submission of the municipality’s financial statements for CY 2012 in compliance with Section 41(2) and (3) of P.D. 1445 in order to provide financial information necessary to guide the decision makers of the LGU as well as to provide information for monitoring by the Department of Finance and the DBM and to provide basis for preparing the annual audit report by the Auditor concerned.

Enforce withholding payment of salaries of persons who cause the failure in the submission of the required year-end financial statements to facilitate report preparation and submission and to avoid possible sanctions by DBM.
observations.managementAction: Issued Memorandum to the Municipal Accountant for the timely submission of monthly trial balance.
observations.reason:
The required reports were not submitted within the reglementary period.

Observation reiterated in the CY 2014 AAR.
